name: Point Transform TOP
description: The Point Transform TOP treats the RGB values of the input image as a point cloud of XYZ positions or vectors and performs 3D transformations and alignments. When the input type is set to 'Vector', translations are ignored and only rotation and scaling operations are performed. The alpha channel, if present, is passed along to the output image unchanged.
summary: The Point Transform TOP treats the RGB values of the input image as a point cloud of XYZ positions or vectors and performs 3D transformations and alignments. When the input type is set to 'Vector', tr

parameters.name: Input Type
parameters.label: Input Type
parameters.description: Choose if the RGB channels of the input texture should be treated as positions or vectors.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: position - The RGB values represent XYZ positions. vector - The RGB values represent XYZ directions.

parameters.name: Normalize Input
parameters.label: Normalize Input
parameters.description: innormalize - RGB input vectors are rescaled to a length of one before they are transformed.

parameters.name: Normalize Output
parameters.label: Normalize Output
parameters.description: outnormalize - RGB vectors are rescaled to a length of one after they are transformed.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description:
xord -  - Changes the order that the translate, rotate and scale operations are performed on the input. Analogous to how you would end up in different locations if you were to move a block and turn east, versus turning east and then moving a block.  In matrix math terms, if we use the 'multiply vector on the right' (column vector) convention, a transform order of Scale, Rotate, Translate would be written as T * R * S * Position

 srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.label: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.description: srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description:
rord -  - As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the rotations take place will alter the final position and orientation. A Rotation order of Rx Ry Rz would create the final rotation matrix as follows R = Rz * Ry * Rx

 xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.label: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.description: xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description:
t -  - Move the input positions in the X, Y and Z axes. If the input is set to 'Vector', the translate values will have no effect.

 tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description:
r -  - Rotate the input RGB values around the corresponding X, Y and Z axes. Angles are given in degrees.

 rx - ry - rz -

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
s -  - Scale the input RGB values in the corresponding X, Y and Z axes. If 'Normalize Output' is on, then all output values will be rescaled to a length of one regardless of the scale values.

 sx - sy - sz -

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description:
p -  - The pivot is the point about which the input points or vectors are scaled and rotated. Altering the pivot point produces different results depending on the transformation performed on the object.

 px - py - pz -

parameters.name: Uniform Scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: scale - Scale the input values along all axes simultaneously.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description: invert - Invert the transformation i.e. preform the reverse movements.

parameters.name: Look At
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: lookat - Allows you to orient your input points by naming the object you would like them to Look At, or point to. Once you have designated this object to look at, it will continue to face that object, even if you move it.

parameters.name: Up Vector
parameters.label: Up Vector
parameters.description:
upvector -  - When orienting an object towards the 'Look At' target, the Up Vector is used to determine where the positive Y axis points.

 upvectorx - upvectory - upvectorz -

parameters.name: Forward Direction
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description:
forwarddir -  - Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

 posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: +X
parameters.label: +X
parameters.description: posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: Transform CHOP
parameters.label: Transform CHOP
parameters.description: chopinput -  to a  node with channels describing a 3D transformation. These channels may come from a Transform CHOP or another  with the correct channels defined.

parameters.name: Multiply Order
parameters.label: Multiply Order
parameters.description:
multiplyorder -  - Controls whether the transformation from the given  is applied to the input values before or after the transformation describe by this node.

 inputxformpage - xformpageinput -

parameters.name: Input, then Transform Page
parameters.label: Input, then Transform Page
parameters.description: inputxformpage - xformpageinput -

parameters.name: Weight Channel
parameters.label: Weight Channel
parameters.description:
weightchannel -  - Select how to use the colors of the second input image as weights for transforming the points of the first input.

 luminance - red - green - blue - alpha - rgbaverage - average - rgbmax - max - independent -

parameters.name: Luminance
parameters.label: Luminance
parameters.description: luminance - red - green - blue - alpha - rgbaverage - average - rgbmax - max - independent -

parameters.name: Weight Range
parameters.label: Weight Range
parameters.description:
weightrange -  - Set the range of weight values used to control how much of the transformation is applied to a point. Points with the minimum weight will not be transformed, while points with the maximum weight will be fully transformed. A linear interpolation is applied to points with weights that fall between the minimum and maximum.

 weightrange1 - weightrange2 -

parameters.name: Align Transform Order
parameters.label: Align Transform Order
parameters.description:
alignxformorder -  - Determines the order that align operations are performed on the input points. Note: Unlike Scaling on the transform page, the alignment scale is always done relative to the center of the point cloud so that the point cloud's center does not change.

 transformalign - aligntransform -

parameters.name: Transform, then Align
parameters.label: Transform, then Align
parameters.description: transformalign - aligntransform -

parameters.name: Reference Node
parameters.label: Reference Node
parameters.description: alignref - A path to a  or  node used to align the input points after the transformation. Note Using another point cloud  as a reference will incur additional performance costs because of the need to calculate the dimensions of the reference points.

parameters.name: Align Operation Order
parameters.label: Align Operation Order
parameters.description:
alignopord -  - Set the order in which scale and transform is applied when aligning.

 st - ts -

parameters.name: Scale Translate
parameters.label: Scale Translate
parameters.description: st - ts -

parameters.name: Align Translate X
parameters.label: Align Translate X
parameters.description:
aligntx -  - Determines the final position of points along the X axis i.e. shifts values in the red channel.

 off - X values are not moved. origin - X values are aligned relative to the origin i.e. zero. reference - X values are aligned relative to the X position of the reference node.

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: off - X values are not moved. origin - X values are aligned relative to the origin i.e. zero. reference - X values are aligned relative to the X position of the reference node.

parameters.name: From Input
parameters.label: From Input
parameters.description:
fromx -  - Determines how the points are aligned relative to the dimensions of the input points.

 min - Points are aligned relative to the lowest X value. center - Points are aligned relative to the center of the X values. max - Points are aligned relative to the highest X value.

parameters.name: Min
parameters.label: Min
parameters.description: min - Points are aligned relative to the lowest X value. center - Points are aligned relative to the center of the X values. max - Points are aligned relative to the highest X value.

parameters.name: To Reference
parameters.label: To Reference
parameters.description:
tox -  - Determines how the final points are aligned relative to the reference node.

 min - Points are aligned with the lowest X value in the reference node. center - Points are aligned with the center of the X values in the reference node. max - Points are aligned with the highest X value in the reference node.

parameters.name: Align Translate Y
parameters.label: Align Translate Y
parameters.description:
alignty -  - Determines the final position of points along the Y axis i.e. shifts values in the green channel.

 off - Y values are not moved. origin - Y values are aligned relative to the origin i.e. zero. reference - Y values are aligned relative to the X position of the reference node.

parameters.name: Align Translate Z
parameters.label: Align Translate Z
parameters.description:
aligntz -  - Determines the final position of points along the Z axis i.e. shifts values in the blue channel.

 off - Z values are not moved. origin - Z values are aligned relative to the origin i.e. zero. reference - Z values are aligned relative to the X position of the reference node.

parameters.name: Align Scale
parameters.label: Align Scale
parameters.description:
alignscale -  - The Align Scale can be used to resize the point cloud to fit inside the given bounds. Scaling can be done per axis (maintaining proportions or stretching), or on all axes.

 peraxis - Scaling is controlled separately per-axis using the parameters below. Note: It is possible to have conflicting scales when setting limits on multiple axes. unity - The point cloud is resized to fit within a 1x1x1 cube. Proportions are maintained so that the largest dimension will have a length of 1. reference - The point cloud is resized to fit within the dimensions of the reference object. Proportions are maintained.

parameters.name: Per Axis
parameters.label: Per Axis
parameters.description: peraxis - Scaling is controlled separately per-axis using the parameters below. Note: It is possible to have conflicting scales when setting limits on multiple axes. unity - The point cloud is resized to fit within a 1x1x1 cube. Proportions are maintained so that the largest dimension will have a length of 1. reference - The point cloud is resized to fit within the dimensions of the reference object. Proportions are maintained.

parameters.name: Align Scale X
parameters.label: Align Scale X
parameters.description:
alignscalex -  - The point cloud is resized based on its width in the X axis.

 off - No scaling is done based on the X axis. unity - The point cloud is resized along the X axis so that the total width is 1. This does not affect the other axes and will distort the overall shape of the cloud. reference - The point cloud is resized along the X axis to the width of the reference object. This does not affect the other axes and will distort the overall shape of the cloud. unityprop - The point cloud is resized so that the total width in the X axis is 1. Other axes are scaled accordingly to maintain proportions. referenceprop - The point cloud is resized to match the width of the reference object. Other axes are scaled accordingly to maintain proportions. unityprop -

parameters.name: Align Scale Y
parameters.label: Align Scale Y
parameters.description:
alignscaley -  - The point cloud is resized based on its height in the Y axis.

 off - No scaling is done based on the Y axis. unity - The point cloud is resized along the Y axis so that the total height is 1. This does not affect the other axes and will distort the overall shape of the cloud. reference - The point cloud is resized along the Y axis to the height of the reference object. This does not affect the other axes and will distort the overall shape of the cloud. unityprop - The point cloud is resized so that the total height in the Y axis is 1. Other axes are scaled accordingly to maintain proportions. referenceprop - The point cloud is resized to match the height of the reference object. Other axes are scaled accordingly to maintain proportions.

parameters.name: Align Scale Z
parameters.label: Align Scale Z
parameters.description:
alignscalez -  - The point cloud is resized based on its depth in the Z axis.

 off - No scaling is done based on the Z axis. unity - The point cloud is resized along the Z axis so that the total depth is 1. This does not affect the other axes and will distort the overall shape of the cloud. reference - The point cloud is resized along the Z axis to the depth of the reference object. This does not affect the other axes and will distort the overall shape of the cloud. unityprop - The point cloud is resized so that the total depth in the Z axis is 1. Other axes are scaled accordingly to maintain proportions. referenceprop - The point cloud is resized to match the depth of the reference object. Other axes are scaled accordingly to maintain proportions.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. Making this larger than 1 is essentially the same as taking the output from each pass, and passing it into the first input of the node and repeating the process. Other inputs and parameters remain the same for each pass.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
